text: TimeSeriesAnalysis transcriptomic analysis tool RNA sequencing microarray data

TimeSeries (TS) analysis visualization package RNAseq microarray data. TS extracts significant genes time course transcriptomic data performing differential gene expression conditional temporal axes. employs partitioning algorithm based recursive thresholding (PART) clustering identify small genomic clusters relevance, followed running clusters gprofiler reveal biological relevance cluster. TS performs: data normalization processing PCA plots Differential gene expression (conditional temporal) PART clustering Heatmaps Trajectory identified clusters Gprofiler (functional enrichment) analysis clusters Dotplots MDS plots Gprofiler results Nearest ancestor clustering GOs GO ancestor queries
can install development version TimeSeriesAnalysis like : Certain bioconductor packages may installed installation TimeSeriesAnalysis. can done following code snippet:
code: install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("Ylefol/TimeSeriesAnalysis") if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))   install.packages("BiocManager") bio_pkgs <- c(PACKAGES HERE) BiocManager::install(bio_pkgs)

text: TimeSeriesAnalysis developed user-friendliness mind, core script package Rmarkdown file explanations code block performs. Computationally intensive code blocks save work order avoid loss computation time. TimeSeriesAnalysis comes test dataset. Users recommended first run package test data modify Rmarkdown script necessary information purposes (input files, organism, genes interest etc…). rmarkdown script expected result example run can found downloaded repository, located ‘rmarkdown_method’ folder. users prefer script approach, equivalent rmarkdown report provided two scripts, one computation tasks second analyses. two scripts found ‘script_method’ folder.
data used example data generated LEMONAID consortium info data?

Top significant genes also labelled rectangles black text order distinguish genes interest plot created using ggplot2, save plot ggsave() function recommended also recommended use following parameters save plot. dpi=300 width=21 height=19 units='cm'

Genes taken order significance genes lowest adjusted p-value taken first. show_non_sig_interest boolean indicate non-significant genes interest shown.
